description: Signals Intelligence page - A full trading operations desk at /signals for analyzing, filtering, comparing, and acting on mispricing signals with three view modes, detail panel with charts, real-time updates, and batch buy execution.

userStories.title: Add /signals route and SignalsLive skeleton
userStories.description: As a user, I want to navigate to /signals from the header so I can access the signals intelligence page.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /signals added to router.ex rendering WeatherEdgeWeb.SignalsLive
Header navigation in header_component.ex includes 'Signals' link between Dashboard and Analytics
SignalsLive mount subscribes to PubSub topics: signals:new, portfolio:balance_update, portfolio:position_update
Page renders with dashboard_header (balance, wallet_address), placeholder text for filter bar and content area
Assigns initialized: filters (default map), signals (empty list), total_count (0), selected (MapSet.new), view_mode (:table), detail_signal_id (nil), balance (from persistent_term)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Signals.Queries module with filtered signal loading
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a query module that loads deduplicated signals with joins to market_clusters, stations, and positions, applying dynamic filters.
acceptanceCriteria: Create lib/weather_edge/signals/queries.ex with defmodule WeatherEdge.Signals.Queries
list_filtered_signals(filters, opts) returns list of maps with keys: signal, cluster, station, position, hours_to_resolution
Joins: Signal -> MarketCluster (on market_cluster_id), Signal -> Station (on station_code), left_join Position (on market_cluster_id + outcome_label + status open)
Base filter: mc.resolved == false
Dynamic filters applied via Ecto.Query.dynamic: stations (list of codes), min_edge (float), side (YES/NO/all), max_price (float or nil), alert_level (string or all)
resolution_date filter: 'today' filters mc.target_date == today, 'tomorrow' == today+1, '+2d' == today+2, '+3d' == today+3, 'all' no filter
has_position filter: 'with_position' requires p.id not nil, 'without_position' requires p.id is nil
actionable_only filter: when true, filters s.market_price <= st.max_buy_price
Deduplication: uses a subquery to get latest signal per (station_code, outcome_label, market_cluster_id) by computed_at desc
Sort options applied via order_by: edge_desc, edge_asc, model_prob_desc, time_to_resolution (asc), price_asc, newest (computed_at desc)
count_filtered_signals(filters) returns integer count with same filters but no limit/offset
Pagination via limit/offset opts (default limit: 20, offset: 0)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build view mode toggle and wire filter state into SignalsLive
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between Table, Grouped, and Heatmap views and have filter state managed in the LiveView.
acceptanceCriteria: Three toggle buttons rendered in SignalsLive: Table (default), Grouped, Heatmap with active state highlighted
handle_event 'set_view' updates view_mode assign to :table, :grouped, or :heatmap
Default filters map defined: %{stations: [], min_edge: 8, resolution_date: 'all', side: 'all', max_price: nil, alert_level: 'all', sort_by: 'edge_desc', actionable_only: false, has_position: 'all'}
On mount (after connected?), signals loaded via Signals.Queries.list_filtered_signals with default filters
View mode persists during filter changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Filter Bar UI component
userStories.description: As a user, I want filter controls to narrow down signals by station, edge, date, side, price, and alert level.
acceptanceCriteria: Filter bar rendered as sticky div at top of signals content area
Station multi-select: phx-change dropdown listing active station codes
Edge minimum: number input or range (0-60, default 8)
Resolution date: select with options All/Today/Tomorrow/+2d/+3d
Side: select with All/YES/NO
Max price: optional number input
Alert level: select with All/Extreme/Strong/Opportunity/Safe NO
Sort: select with Highest Edge/Lowest Price/Highest Model Prob/Soonest Resolution/Newest
Actionable Only: checkbox toggle
Has Position: select with All/With Position/Without Position
Status line shows: 'Active: N filters | Showing X of Y signals' with Clear All button
Each filter change sends phx-change event, handler re-queries via Signals.Queries and resets pagination
Clear All resets to default filters
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build price trend helper from market_snapshots
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function to compute price direction over the last 6 hours for the trend column.
acceptanceCriteria: Add price_trend(cluster_id, outcome_label, opts) to WeatherEdge.Markets module or a new MarketSnapshots helper
Query market_snapshots for the cluster_id + outcome_label in the last N hours (default 6)
Returns {direction, delta} where direction is :up, :down, or :flat
Direction computed: if newest yes_price > oldest yes_price -> :up, if less -> :down, else :flat
Delta is newest - oldest (float)
Returns {:flat, 0.0} when fewer than 2 snapshots available
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build Table View with signal rows
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see signals in a table with columns for time, station, outcome, resolution, action, alert, confidence, prices, edge, volume, trend, and position.
acceptanceCriteria: Table rendered when view_mode == :table inside SignalsLive render

Columns: checkbox (phx-click toggle_select), Time (HH:MM from computed_at), Station (ICAO code), Temp (outcome_label), Resolves (hours from hours_to_resolution, red text if <6, 'Today' badge if resolves today), Action (BUY YES green / BUY NO blue / BOUGHT purple badge if position exists), Alert (color badge matching existing alert_class), Confidence, Market ($ price), Model (% probability), Edge (% bold, green if >15%, yellow if >8%), Volume (from cluster outcomes JSONB liquidity), Trend (arrow + delta from price_trend), Pos? (token count or dash)
Each row has phx-click='open_detail' with signal id
Station code has phx-click to filter by that station
Show more button at bottom loads next 20 signals via handle_event 'load_more'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build row selection and Quick Actions Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select signals via checkboxes and see a sticky bottom bar with batch buy controls.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_event 'toggle_select' toggles signal id in MapSet assigns.selected
Sticky bar at bottom of page always visible with: balance display, selected count, total estimated cost (sum of station buy_amount_usdc for selected), BUY ALL N button
BUY ALL button disabled when selected is empty
handle_event 'buy_selected' validates: balance >= cost + reserve, no duplicate positions, max 10 selected
On validation failure, flash error message
On success, executes orders sequentially via Trading.OrderManager or ClobClient for each selected signal
Progress shown: 'Buying 1/3...' updated via send(self(), {:buy_progress, n})
After completion, flash success, clear selection, reload signals
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Signal Detail Panel slide-in (data and layout)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a signal row and see full context in a slide-in panel from the right.
acceptanceCriteria: Create WeatherEdge.Signals.DetailData module with fetch_signal_detail(signal_id) that returns map with: signal, cluster, distribution (from Engine.compute_distribution), model_breakdown (from Forecasts.latest_snapshots), orderbook (from ClobClient.get_orderbook), metar (from MetarClient if resolves today/tomorrow), position (open position for this outcome if any), balance
Panel renders as fixed right div (w-2/5) with z-50, slide-in transition, when detail_signal_id is not nil
Close button (X) and click-outside handler set detail_signal_id to nil
Panel header: station code, outcome label, target date, edge %, alert badge, confidence badge
Cluster distribution section: list of all outcomes showing model prob bar (blue) vs market price bar (orange) side by side, with edge delta text
Model breakdown section: list each model name + max_temp_c, consensus count at bottom
Orderbook section: best bid price+size, best ask price+size, spread
METAR section (if metar data): current temp, today's max observed
Position section (if position): tokens, avg_buy_price, current_price, unrealized P&L
Open on Polymarket link using cluster.event_slug
Data fetched asynchronously via send(self(), {:load_detail, signal_id}) pattern to avoid blocking
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add buy controls to Detail Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to buy directly from the detail panel with configurable amount.
acceptanceCriteria: Amount input in detail panel, defaults to station's buy_amount_usdc
Display: estimated tokens (amount / market_price), payout if wins (tokens * 1.0), return percentage ((payout - amount) / amount * 100)
BUY YES and BUY NO buttons
handle_event 'buy_from_detail' executes real order via Trading.OrderManager.place_signal_order or ClobClient
Success: flash info message, reload detail data to show updated position
Error: flash error message with reason
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Chart.js hooks and edge/price history queries
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Chart.js integrated via LiveView hooks and the backend queries for edge history and price history.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart.js loaded in app.js or via CDN script tag in root layout
LiveView JS hook 'ChartHook' created in app.js: on mounted(), reads data from el.dataset, creates Chart.js instance; on updated(), updates chart data and calls chart.update()
Cluster distribution chart: horizontal bar chart with two datasets (model prob blue, market price orange) for each outcome label
Add Signals.edge_history(station_code, cluster_id, outcome_label, hours: 24) query returning list of %{time, edge, model_prob, market_price}
Add MarketSnapshots.price_history(cluster_id, outcome_label, hours: 48) query returning list of %{time, yes_price}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render charts in Detail Panel using Chart.js hooks
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see edge history and price history charts in the detail panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Edge History chart rendered in detail panel: line chart, time on X, edge % on Y, last 24h data from Signals.edge_history
Price History chart rendered: line chart, time on X, YES price on Y, last 48h from MarketSnapshots.price_history, buy price horizontal marker if position exists
Cluster Distribution chart rendered: horizontal bar using cluster outcomes model_prob vs yes_price
Charts use phx-hook='ChartHook' with data passed via data attributes (JSON encoded)
Charts update when detail panel reloads data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Grouped View with event cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see signals grouped by event with best play, hedge options, and cluster health.
acceptanceCriteria: Create WeatherEdge.Signals.GroupedView module with group_signals_by_event(signals) function
Groups signals by {station_code, market_cluster_id}

Each group struct: station_code, cluster, best_play (highest-edge YES signal), hedge_options (safe_no signals sorted by edge desc), other_signals (remaining), cluster_health (sum of yes_prices), forecast_consensus (count of models agreeing on predicted temp from latest_snapshots), hours_to_resolution, signal_count
Rendered when view_mode == :grouped: collapsible cards sorted by soonest resolution
Card header: station code, city, target date, hours to resolution
Cluster Health line: sum YES with warning badge if deviation > 5%
BEST PLAY section: highlighted signal with payout calculation (tokens * 1.0 - cost)
HEDGE OPTIONS section: list of safe_no signals
OTHER section: remaining signals
Buttons: BUY BEST (buys best_play), BUY BEST + HEDGE (buys best_play + top hedge), VIEW FULL CLUSTER (opens detail panel)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Heatmap View
userStories.description: As a user, I want a grid overview showing best edge per station per date to spot opportunities instantly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create WeatherEdge.Signals.HeatmapData module with build_heatmap(signals) function
Returns list of maps: station_code, city, date, date_label (Today/Tomorrow/+2d/+3d), best_edge, signal_count, has_position, has_event
Dates: today through +3d computed dynamically
Rendered when view_mode == :heatmap: grid table with station rows, date columns
Cell color intensity: gray (<8%), light green (8-15%), medium green (15-25%), dark green/red (25%+), empty if no event
Cell text shows best edge percentage
Position indicator: small dot if has_position is true
Click cell sets filters to station + date and switches to table view
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Build Performance Tracker section
userStories.description: As a user, I want historical signal accuracy and P&L stats at the bottom of the page.
acceptanceCriteria: Create WeatherEdge.Signals.Performance module with compute_stats(opts) function
Stats computed from resolved positions + accuracy records over last N days (default 30)
Returns: total_signals, accuracy (correct/total), accuracy_by_level (map of alert_level -> %{accuracy, count}), accuracy_by_station (map of station_code -> %{accuracy, pnl}), total_pnl, avg_edge
Signal history list: date, station, temp, action (side), edge, result (won/lost/sold), pnl, status
Collapsible section at bottom of page, collapsed by default
handle_event 'toggle_performance' expands/collapses, loads data lazily on first expand
Summary stats row: overall accuracy %, avg edge, total P&L, signals followed count
Breakdown by alert level table
Breakdown by station table
Signal history table with past signals and outcomes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Wire real-time PubSub updates into SignalsLive
userStories.description: As a user, I want new signals to appear live and balance/position updates reflected in real-time.
acceptanceCriteria: handle_info for {:new_signal, signal}: prepend to signals list if it matches current filters, increment total_count, add CSS highlight class that fades after 3s
handle_info for {:balance_updated, balance}: update balance assign (reflected in header and quick actions bar)
handle_info for {:position_updated, position}: update matching signal rows to show BOUGHT badge and position token count
Stale signal detection: if a signal's market_price differs >10% from the cluster's current outcome yes_price, show warning icon on that row
Typecheck passes
